creator: 75394094@qq.com
text:
If system boots normally, rc script is not necessary to determine rc_pid, and it can boot even faster. Is it right ?

Fix: 

-       eval $_pidcmd                   # determine the pid if necessary
+       [ -z "$autoboot" ] && eval $_pidcmd                     # determine the pid if necessary

        for _elem in $_keywords; do
                if [ "$_elem" != "$rc_arg" ]; then--utHNY7dhjQoOMuoEo1sDyGTMLN5dHbZo3QP7sFIok42I8cqR
Content-Type: text/plain; name="file.diff"
Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit
Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="file.diff"

--- /etc/rc.subr.orig   2010-07-19 10:24:59.000000000 +0800
+++ /etc/rc.subr        2010-09-20 16:44:26.000000000 +0800
@@ -657,7 +657,7 @@
                fi
        fi
How-To-Repeat: reboot your system, you can add one line(line: set -x ) into /etc/rc to view, and you can see _find_process (invoked by check_pidfile or check_process) to be executed.
